title: claim-next cross-lane fallback skips lanes actively owned by another live session (GH #20)
action: In claimNextCell (canonical skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs, cross-lane fallback pool ~:899-933): before pooling a lane's ready cells, skip the lane when at least one session record other than the acting session has lane === that feature AND !heartbeatStale(record) (reuse exported helper + DEFAULT_HEARTBEAT_STALE_SECONDS from claims.mjs; add a tiny list-sessions reader there only if none exists). Own-lane-first branch, direct claim-by-id, and the default state.json pipeline are untouched. Preserve D4 zero-lane/zero-session parity. Add fsh-11 test rows: (1) live-owner lane excluded from pool even when backlog-ranked first -> falls to unowned lane; (2) stale-owner lane stays poolable; (3) acting session's own binding never excludes its own lane; (4) no-session-records parity intact. Sync byte mirrors .bee/bin/lib/, .claude/skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/, .agents/skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/ for every edited lib file. Do NOT touch .claude-plugin/, .codex-plugin/, or docs/history/codex-harness-hardening/release-manifest.json — those paths are held by another live session; the orchestrator finishes them separately.

trace.outcome: claimNextCell's cross-lane fallback pool now skips any lane actively owned by another live session (fresh heartbeat), closing GH#20 (claim-next stealing a freshly planned lane out from another session)
trace.verify_output: 347 passed, 0 failed | test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib are byte-identical (19 files); runtime-derived hook inventory is byte-identical (10 files)
